dashboard.description: Overview of business status and key statistics
dashboard.goal: The main dashboard gives you a fast, comprehensive picture of your business status: files, projects, clients, revenue, and recent activity.

tips.0: Statistics update automatically — no manual refresh needed
tips.1: Charts are visible to managers only
tips.2: Use the quick actions at the bottom of the page to upload files or create a project quickly
tips.3: Click any stat card to jump straight to its page

dashboard_notifications.goal: Keep track of everything happening in the workspace: new comments, pending approvals, project updates, and financial alerts.

tips.0: Unread notifications appear with a highlighted background
tips.1: You can mark all as read from the button at the top of the page
tips.2: Click a notification to jump straight to the related item

dashboard_files.description: Upload, organize, and share files and folders
dashboard_files.goal: A complete file repository for organizing all project and client files. Supports file uploads, folder creation, link sharing, and permission management.

tips.0: Drag and drop files directly to upload them
tips.1: Double-click to open folders
tips.2: You can share any file via an external link
tips.3: Add important files to favorites with one click
tips.4: Deleted files go to Trash and can be restored

dashboard_favorites.goal: Quick access to your most-used files and folders. Add any item to favorites from the file browser.

tips.0: Add a file to favorites by clicking the star icon
tips.1: Favorites are personal — each user has their own list
tips.2: Click an item to open it directly in the file browser

dashboard_reviews.goal: Manage the file review cycle: request a review from the team, track status, and approve or reject files.

tips.0: You can request a review from any file's page
tips.1: Pending reviews appear first
tips.2: Add comments on the review to clarify feedback

dashboard_trash.goal: Browse deleted files and restore them when needed. Files stay in Trash until permanently deleted.

tips.0: You can restore any file with one click
tips.1: Permanent deletion cannot be undone
tips.2: Use search to quickly find a deleted file

dashboard_storage.goal: An analytics dashboard for monitoring storage usage: total size, breakdown by file type, and the largest folders and files.

tips.0: The pie chart shows the breakdown by file type (images, video, PDF, etc.)
tips.1: The bar chart shows the 10 largest folders by size
tips.2: The largest-files table helps you identify which files consume the most space
tips.3: Statistics refresh automatically on every visit

dashboard_projects.description: Manage projects, track progress and tasks
dashboard_projects.goal: The project management hub: create new projects, assign the team, set a budget, and track progress with stages and percentages.

tips.0: Create a new project from the "New Project" button at the top of the page
tips.1: Link the project to a client to track revenue and expenses
tips.2: Updating the completion percentage shows up automatically in reports
tips.3: Add a budget to the project to track budget usage in financial reports

dashboard_quotes.goal: Create professional quotes with detailed line items, send them to clients for e-signature, and convert them to invoices with one click.

tips.0: Use the quote builder to add line items with prices and quantities
tips.1: The quote total is calculated automatically (subtotal + tax + total)
tips.2: The client can sign electronically through the portal
tips.3: Convert an accepted quote directly into an invoice
tips.4: Export the quote as a PDF to share it

dashboard_quotes_analytics.goal: Track conversion rates, the value of active quotes, signing speed, and sales agent performance.

tips.0: The conversion funnel shows the percentage of quotes that move from sent to signed to invoiced
tips.1: Average days to sign measures how fast clients respond
tips.2: Agent performance helps evaluate the sales team
tips.3: The value of active quotes gives a picture of the expected pipeline

dashboard_invoices.description: Issue and track invoices and payments
dashboard_invoices.goal: Create professional invoices, track payment status, and send reminders to clients. Supports online payment via Stripe.

tips.0: Create an invoice from scratch or convert an accepted quote
tips.1: Invoice statuses: draft → sent → partially paid → paid
tips.2: The client can pay online through the client portal
tips.3: Export the invoice as a PDF with full Arabic support
tips.4: Overdue invoices show alerts on the dashboard

dashboard_clients.description: Manage client accounts and their portal
dashboard_clients.goal: A central point for creating and editing client accounts, managing tags, exporting data, and tracking financial relationships and projects.

tips.0: Use search and tags to find a client quickly
tips.1: Switch between table and card views from the toolbar
tips.2: Click the client's name to go to their full detail page
tips.3: Export client data as CSV from the export button
tips.4: Add colored tags to organize clients by type or sector

dashboard_clients_id.goal: A comprehensive view of client information: projects, invoices, quotes, notes, activity, and branding.

tips.0: Use notes to document meetings and important decisions
tips.1: Track each client's outstanding balance from the stat cards
tips.2: Add tags to clients to make filtering and organizing easier
tips.3: Customize the client portal's branding from the Branding tab

dashboard_script_reviews.goal: Manage the content script review cycle: from submission through review to final approval.

tips.0: Upload the script and assign reviewers
tips.1: Track review status from the list
tips.2: Add notes and edits to each script

dashboard_guide.description: Comprehensive guide to all system modules
dashboard_guide.goal: A reference guide explaining every module in the system with tips and goals. Use search to quickly find any module.

tips.0: Use search to find any module in Arabic or English
tips.1: Click any card to view usage tips
tips.2: Click the link icon to go straight to the module

dashboard_profile.goal: Update your personal information, change your password, and view your role and permissions in the system.

tips.0: You can upload a profile picture that appears throughout the system
tips.1: Changing your password requires at least 8 characters
tips.2: Your permissions are view-only — contact the admin to change them
tips.3: You can review your active sessions and end any suspicious session

dashboard_boards.description: Task management boards — Kanban, Pipeline & List views
dashboard_boards.goal: A unified task management system supporting 3 views: Kanban (drag and drop), Pipeline (sequential stages with % progress), and List (a sortable table). Each board can be linked to a project and a team.

tips.0: Choose between Kanban or Pipeline when creating the board — you can change it later from Settings
tips.1: 6 ready-made templates: General, Content, Design, Campaigns, Video, Social Media
tips.2: The Pipeline view calculates the progress percentage automatically and shows it with a progress bar
tips.3: Use the toolbar to filter by members, labels, priority, or due date
tips.4: Click + at the bottom of a column to add a quick task without opening a dialog
tips.5: Click any card to open the task details: description, attachments, @mention comments, and checklist
tips.6: Pipeline boards support Stage Gates — stages that require approval before moving on
tips.7: The List view supports multi-select + bulk actions (move, priority, archive)
tips.8: Updates appear instantly via Supabase Realtime — no manual refresh needed

dashboard_my_tasks.description: Personal task dashboard — all tasks assigned to you
dashboard_my_tasks.goal: See all tasks assigned to you across every board and project in one place. Automatic grouping by date, with the option to group by board, priority, or project.

tips.0: Tasks are auto-categorized: overdue, today, this week, upcoming, completed
tips.1: Use the grouping buttons (date | board | priority | project) for a different view
tips.2: Pipeline boards show a dedicated progress bar for completion percentage
tips.3: Click any task to go straight to its board
tips.4: Overdue tasks appear in red with a counter in the statistics

dashboard_directory.goal: View all team members with their contact information and roles. You can search and filter by team or role.

tips.0: Click an employee's card to view their details
tips.1: Use the team filter to view members of a specific team
tips.2: Roles are shown as colored badges based on the color set in the role's settings

dashboard_hr.goal: A quick, comprehensive overview of HR health: today's attendance, paid-leave liability, payroll cost, active evaluations, urgent alerts, and upcoming events — all in one admin-only dashboard.

tips.0: This page is admin-only and requires the hr.view permission — regular employees don't see this dashboard.
tips.1: The "Today's Attendance Rate" card shows how many are present out of total employees + the live percentage.
tips.2: The "Paid Leave Liability" card automatically calculates the monetary value of accrued unused days by multiplying the daily salary × leave balance — useful for monthly financial planning.
tips.3: The "Monthly Payroll Cost" card shows the actual spend for the current month with a 6-month trend chart.
tips.4: The "Active Evaluations" section shows currently open evaluation periods — click to go to /dashboard/evaluations to manage evaluations.
tips.5: The "Alerts" section collects urgent cases: employees without a direct manager, leave requests pending for a long time, and contracts nearing expiry.
tips.6: The "Celebrations" section shows upcoming birthdays and work anniversaries in the next seven days — useful for team morale and culture.
tips.7: All figures are computed in real time from the database — no manual refresh needed.
tips.8: To go to the details of any section (attendance, leave, payroll, evaluations), use the quick links under each card.

dashboard_hr_productivity.description: Monthly production-team productivity report — deliveries, on-time rate, revision rounds, speed, and attendance
dashboard_hr_productivity.goal: Find out at month-end who delivered what, how late they were, and whose work keeps getting sent back for revision.

tips.0: Pick the month from the month picker at the top of the page — defaults to the current month (Dubai time)
tips.1: "On-time rate" is measured against the FIRST submission for review — not the final delivery — so your review time doesn't count against the employee
tips.2: "Waiting on your review" is YOUR number: the average time from a version being submitted to your decision (approve/request changes)
tips.3: Click "Task Details" under any employee for a table of every task and its journey — every number on the cards is traceable to its source
tips.4: "Overdue, not submitted" = tasks that passed their deadline without any review version — this is the first thing to ask about
tips.5: All figures are derived from the task stage-movement log (pyra_task_stage_history) — moves via buttons or drag-and-drop are both recorded

dashboard_approvals.description: Team leave, expense, and timesheet approvals
dashboard_approvals.goal: One place to review all requests from your direct reports: leave, expenses, and timesheets. Approve or reject with a single click.

tips.0: Here you only see requests from employees under your direct management
tips.1: Admin sees every request in the system
tips.2: Rejecting requires a reason so the employee understands and can fix it
tips.3: As soon as you approve a leave request, the balance is deducted automatically
tips.4: The badge next to the name counts all pending requests across the 3 types

dashboard_timesheet.description: Track work hours, periods, and overtime
dashboard_timesheet.goal: Log your daily work hours linked to projects and tasks. Group hours into weekly or monthly periods and submit them for approval. Overtime is detected automatically.

tips.0: Log your hours daily for accurate reports
tips.1: Link hours to specific tasks for more precise tracking
tips.2: Submit your weekly period for approval before the end of the week
tips.3: Overtime is calculated automatically based on your work schedule
tips.4: The overtime summary appears as a separate card with the approved multiplier
tips.5: You can review your monthly totals in reports

dashboard_announcements.goal: Follow company announcements and the latest updates. Urgent announcements appear as a banner at the top of the dashboard.

tips.0: Pinned announcements always appear at the top
tips.1: Unread announcements are shown with special highlighting
tips.2: Admins can create and edit announcements
tips.3: An announcement can be targeted to specific teams

dashboard_leave.goal: Submit leave requests and track their status. View your leave balance by custom leave type, with support for annual balance carry-over.

tips.0: Check your balance before submitting a request
tips.1: Add a clear reason to speed up approval
tips.2: You can cancel a pending request, or even an approved leave, by stating a reason
tips.3: The system automatically detects overlapping team leave when you create a new request
tips.4: The balance carried over from the previous year appears on each leave type's card
tips.5: The team calendar shows who is on leave

dashboard_leave_settings.goal: Create and edit custom leave types (annual, sick, personal, and others) with a default balance and carry-over policy.

tips.0: Set the default number of days for each leave type
tips.1: Enable automatic carry-over with a maximum cap
tips.2: Some leave types require attachments (like sick leave)
tips.3: Order leave types to display them in the desired sequence

dashboard_hr_leave_balances.description: View and adjust employee leave balances by year — admin only
dashboard_hr_leave_balances.goal: Review every employee's balance for each leave type in a given year (total, used, carried over, and remaining) and adjust it manually when needed — e.g. correcting a balance after an admin error or granting extra days.

tips.0: Pick the year from the dropdown at the top of the page (previous, current, or next) — the table updates instantly for the selected year.
tips.1: Every active employee (employee or sales agent) appears in the table even if they have no recorded balance yet for this year — they show a zero balance.
tips.2: Remaining balance is calculated as: total balance + carried-over days − used days. A red number means a negative balance (over the allowed limit).
tips.3: Click "Edit" next to the employee's name to open a dialog for adjusting their balance across all leave types at once.
tips.4: Balance carry-over from one year to the next happens automatically on January 1st via a scheduled job (cron) — no need to run it manually under normal circumstances.
tips.5: Unpaid leave is not deducted from any balance — only adjust its balance if you want to track days taken for statistical purposes.
tips.6: Any manual balance adjustment is recorded in the activity log for easy review later.
tips.7: The "Leave Settings" page (leave/settings) controls the default balance and carry-over policy for each type — use it to change the defaults for future years.

dashboard_attendance.goal: Clock in and out daily. Track your monthly attendance record with statistics on lateness, absence, and total hours worked.

tips.0: Press the "Clock In" button at the start of your shift
tips.1: Press "Clock Out" at the end of your shift — the time is calculated automatically
tips.2: The monthly calendar shows attendance days in different colors based on status
tips.3: Monthly statistics include: days present, lateness, absences, and total hours
tips.4: Admins can view the attendance of all employees

dashboard_org_chart.goal: A tree view of the organizational structure showing the manager-and-reports chain. Helps you understand the reporting and approval line.

tips.0: The tree starts from the general manager and branches downward
tips.1: Click any employee to view their details
tips.2: The reporting line determines who approves leave and attendance records
tips.3: An admin can set the direct manager from the user settings

dashboard_my_payslips.goal: View your monthly payslips with details of base salary, task payments, overtime, bonuses, and deductions.

tips.0: A payslip becomes available after the monthly payroll run is approved
tips.1: You can download the payslip as a PDF
tips.2: The breakdown includes: base salary + task payments + overtime + bonuses − deductions = net pay

dashboard_finance.goal: An overview of the financial position: revenue, expenses, profit, financial alerts, and a quick summary of every finance section.

tips.0: The top cards show a quick summary of revenue and expenses
tips.1: Financial alerts show overdue invoices and subscriptions nearing expiry
tips.2: Click any section to go to its detail page

dashboard_finance_expenses.goal: Record all company expenses with category and linked project. Supports tax and multiple currencies.

tips.0: Add a new expense from the button at the top of the page
tips.1: Link the expense to a project to calculate project profitability
tips.2: Add the VAT amount for accurate tax reports
tips.3: Use categories to analyze spending patterns
tips.4: You can manage categories from the "Categories" page

dashboard_finance_expenses_categories.goal: Create and edit expense categories to better organize and analyze spending.

tips.0: Create categories that fit the nature of your business
tips.1: Categories are used when creating expenses and in reports
tips.2: You can edit or delete any category

dashboard_finance_client_statement.goal: View a detailed account statement for each client, including invoices, payments, and the remaining balance.

tips.0: Select a client to view their statement
tips.1: The statement includes invoices, payments, and balance
tips.2: You can export the statement as a file

dashboard_finance_cards.goal: Register company cards and track the expenses linked to each card.

tips.0: Add a new card with its details
tips.1: Link expenses to a specific card to track spending
tips.2: Track the card limit and the balance used

dashboard_finance_contracts.goal: Track all contracts: monthly retainers with automatic billing, or milestone contracts that generate an invoice for each completed milestone.

tips.0: A "monthly retainer" contract creates automatic billing once activated
tips.1: Set the monthly amount, the billing cycle, and the billing day
tips.2: Changing the contract status to "active" enables automatic billing
tips.3: Cancelling or completing the contract stops recurring billing
tips.4: A "milestone" contract lets you generate an invoice for each completed milestone
tips.5: Add scope-of-work items from the contract edit page — they appear on invoices and in the portal

dashboard_finance_credit_notes.description: Manage credit notes — refunds and adjustments
dashboard_finance_credit_notes.goal: Create credit notes to adjust or cancel issued invoices, with the option to apply them to reduce the amount due.

tips.0: Create a credit note from the invoice page or directly
tips.1: Credit note statuses: draft → issued → applied or voided
tips.2: Applying the credit note reduces the amount due on the linked invoice
tips.3: The credit note can be linked to a specific invoice, or created unlinked

dashboard_finance_suppliers.description: Manage suppliers and their banking details
dashboard_finance_suppliers.goal: Register the suppliers the company works with, and link them to expenses and purchase orders to track payments.

tips.0: Add the supplier's banking details to make payments easier
tips.1: The supplier can be linked to expenses when creating them
tips.2: Deactivating a supplier hides them from lists without deleting their data
tips.3: Add the Tax Registration Number (TRN) for tax compliance

dashboard_finance_purchase_orders.description: Create and manage purchase orders for suppliers
dashboard_finance_purchase_orders.goal: Issue formal purchase orders to suppliers and track order status from creation through receipt and invoicing.

tips.0: Order statuses: draft → sent → confirmed → received → invoiced
tips.1: Select the supplier from the registry to auto-fill the details
tips.2: The purchase order can be linked to a specific project
tips.3: Purchase orders cannot be deleted after they've been sent

dashboard_finance_recurring.goal: Create invoice templates that are issued automatically on a set schedule (monthly, quarterly, annual).

tips.0: Set the client, line items, and recurrence cycle
tips.1: The invoice is generated automatically on the scheduled date
tips.2: You can pause or edit the recurring invoice at any time

dashboard_finance_reports.description: Detailed financial reports and profitability analytics
dashboard_finance_reports.goal: Comprehensive financial analysis including: profit & loss, cash flow, tax, client profitability, and project profitability.

tips.0: Choose the time period from the date filter
tips.1: The "Profit & Loss" tab shows revenue and expenses for each period
tips.2: The "Cash Flow" tab shows money in (receipts) versus money out (expenses + refunds) month by month, with the cumulative balance and expected collections
tips.3: The "Tax" tab calculates the net VAT due
tips.4: The "Client Profitability" tab ranks clients by profit
tips.5: The "Project Profitability" tab tracks budget usage for each project
tips.6: Export the report as a file to share

dashboard_finance_targets.goal: Set monthly or annual revenue targets and track progress toward achieving them.

tips.0: Set a revenue target for each period
tips.1: The progress bar updates automatically with paid invoices
tips.2: Compare actual performance against the set target

dashboard_payroll.goal: Create a monthly payroll run that combines: base salary + task payments + overtime + bonuses − deductions. Approve it and pay out salaries.

tips.0: Create a new payroll run for each month
tips.1: The "Calculate" button computes all employee line items automatically
tips.2: You can add manual bonuses or deductions for each employee
tips.3: Approve the run, then record the disbursement to issue payslips
tips.4: Export each employee's payslip as a PDF

dashboard_evaluations.goal: Create periodic evaluation cycles and rate employee performance against defined criteria. Track KPIs and achieve goals.

tips.0: Create an evaluation period (quarterly or annual), then add evaluations
tips.1: Each evaluation includes scores across multiple criteria (quality, productivity, communication...)
tips.2: The radar chart shows strengths and areas for improvement
tips.3: Set KPI targets for each employee and track progress — from the "KPIs" tab, click "Update Progress" next to any KPI to record the new actual value
tips.4: The employee sees their evaluation and acknowledges having reviewed it
tips.5: If the employee's rating is 3.5 stars or higher, a "Recommend Bonus" button appears in the evaluation details — it calculates the bonus automatically (5%/10%/15% of salary based on the grade) and creates a pending payment in the employee's salary currency
tips.6: The "Performance Trend" tab shows a line chart of one employee's ratings across all periods + the average + a "low performance" alert if any rating drops below 3.0

dashboard_evaluations_settings.goal: Customize performance evaluation criteria (technical and behavioral) and their weights. Manage evaluation periods and their statuses.

tips.0: Add new criteria and set their weight and category (technical or behavioral)
tips.1: Default criteria: work quality, productivity, communication, teamwork
tips.2: You can disable a criterion without deleting it

dashboard_hr_documents.description: Employee documents management with expiry tracking and alerts — admin only
dashboard_hr_documents.goal: Upload employee documents (passports, work permits, contracts, certificates...) and track their expiry dates. The system sends automatic alerts 30 and 7 days before expiry. Documents are protected with private signed URLs (private bucket).

tips.0: This page is admin-only and requires the documents.manage permission — employees only see their own documents via the "My Documents" page.
tips.1: To upload a document: select the employee → choose the document type from the customizable list → set the expiry date → upload the file.
tips.2: Document types are customizable from system settings — e.g. passport, work permit, residency, employment contract, professional certificate.
tips.3: The system sends an automatic alert 30 days before a document expires, and another 7 days before — no manual follow-up needed.
tips.4: Expired documents show a red "Expired" badge and are collected in the admin's urgent alerts list.
tips.5: All documents are stored in a private bucket and a time-limited signed URL is issued for access — no one can reach the file without permission.
tips.6: Use the "Employee", "Document Type", or "Status" (valid/expiring soon/expired) filter to quickly narrow the list.
tips.7: Click a document's card to preview or download the file — the link is generated on the spot and is valid for one hour.
tips.8: You can edit the expiry date or replace the uploaded file from the same card without deleting and re-uploading the document.

dashboard_hr_documents_settings.description: Manage employee document type catalogue — admin only
dashboard_hr_documents_settings.goal: Customize the list of document types used by the document vault. Add new types, edit existing ones, or disable them without changing any code.

tips.0: Each document type has an Arabic + English name and a "requires expiry date" setting — enable it for types like passport and residency.
tips.1: Disabled types (is_active=false) don't appear in the new-upload list — you can disable a type instead of deleting it to preserve old documents.
tips.2: Default types: employment contract, Emirates ID, passport, residency/visa, certificate, other.
tips.3: Display order is controlled by the sort_order field — smaller numbers appear first.
tips.4: These settings affect the Employee Documents page (/dashboard/hr/documents) and the Onboarding wizard (/dashboard/hr/onboarding).

dashboard_hr_onboarding.description: New hire onboarding — auto-generate offer letter, NDA, asset handover PDF + task checklist
dashboard_hr_onboarding.goal: Automate the new-hire onboarding process: enter personal, job, salary, and asset-custody data → the system creates the account, generates documents (offer letter + NDA + asset handover form), and starts the onboarding task checklist.

tips.0: Click "New Employee" and follow the 5 steps: personal info → job → compensation → clauses & assets → review → complete.
tips.1: Base salary is required — the monthly total is calculated automatically from (base + housing + transport + communication + other allowances).
tips.2: Enable "Sales employee" in the Job step to show the commission rate and monthly target fields in the Compensation step.
tips.3: Custom clauses appear at the end of the offer letter PDF — use them for confidentiality terms or special notes.
tips.4: After onboarding is created, click the row to open the detail page: update checklist tasks, download documents, and formally complete the onboarding.
tips.5: If a document fails to generate, the page shows it as "Not generated yet" with a "Generate" button to retry without recreating the user.
tips.6: To formally complete onboarding: finish every checklist task, then click "Complete Onboarding" on the detail page.
tips.7: This page is for the full onboarding of a new employee with the contract PDF, NDA, and asset handover form — if you just want to create an account without documents, use /dashboard/users directly.
tips.8: The onboarding detail page has a "View Employee Profile" link to open the new employee's account directly in /dashboard/users once onboarding is complete.

dashboard_hr_work_schedules.description: Manage weekly work schedules and assign them to employees
dashboard_hr_work_schedules.goal: Create multiple work schedules (e.g. a regular Monday–Saturday schedule, a seasonal schedule...) and assign each employee to their own schedule. The start time is used automatically to calculate lateness in the attendance record.

tips.0: Click "Add Schedule", then enter the name in Arabic and English, choose the work days, and the start and end times.
tips.1: The default work days for a new schedule are Monday–Saturday (the company's weekend is Sunday only) — change the selection if needed.
tips.2: The start time is used directly to calculate lateness — an employee who clocks in after this time is marked "late" in the attendance record.
tips.3: The default schedule (is_default) is applied automatically to any employee who hasn't been assigned a specific schedule — always keep exactly one default schedule.
tips.4: To assign a schedule to an employee: open /dashboard/users → edit the user → the "Employment Info" section → choose "Work Schedule".
tips.5: The default schedule cannot be deleted — make another schedule the default first before deleting.
tips.6: A schedule assigned to employees cannot be deleted — reassign those employees to another schedule first from the users page.
tips.7: The overtime multiplier and weekend multiplier are used later in payroll calculations.

dashboard_my_documents.description: My documents — self-service view of personal documents uploaded by admin
dashboard_my_documents.goal: View all your personal documents (passport, residency, contract...) uploaded on your behalf by admin. See each document's status and download the file directly. The system reminds you automatically as any document nears expiry.

tips.0: This page shows only your own documents — you won't see your colleagues' documents.
tips.1: Documents are ordered by status: valid → expiring soon → expired.
tips.2: The yellow "Expiring soon" badge means less than 30 days remain — contact admin to renew the document.
tips.3: The red "Expired" badge means it has expired — you're advised to contact admin immediately.
tips.4: Click the document's card or the download button to get a secure (signed URL) link and download the file directly.
tips.5: Generated links are valid for one hour only — if a link expires, click download again for a new one.
tips.6: You'll receive an in-system notification 30 days before, and again 7 days before, any document expires.
tips.7: You cannot upload or edit documents from this page — edits are made by admin only via the "Employee Documents" page.

dashboard_content_pipeline.description: Content production pipeline from script to delivery
dashboard_content_pipeline.goal: Track content production stages: scriptwriting → review → revision → filming → editing → client review → delivery.

tips.0: Create a new content item and set the project and type (video, photos...)
tips.1: The Kanban board shows the stages as sequential columns
tips.2: Assign an owner to each stage to distribute the work
tips.3: Log notes on each stage to document decisions
tips.4: Link the content to a script review for full traceability

dashboard_teams.goal: Organize your staff into work teams with file access permissions per team.

tips.0: Create a new team and add members
tips.1: Each team can access specific folders
tips.2: Assign a lead to each team to manage its members

dashboard_users.goal: Add new users, assign them roles and permissions, and set the management structure (manager → reports) for the approval chain.

tips.0: Create a user: enter a username (≥ 3 characters) + password (≥ 8 characters — the PASSWORD_MIN_LENGTH constant) + display name + role
tips.1: Role has 3 options: admin (everything) / employee (base + self-service HR) / sales_agent (CRM + WhatsApp + everything in employee)
tips.2: manager_username sets the direct manager — used in approvals (leave, expenses, timesheet) via canApproveFor()
tips.3: extra_permissions adds extra permissions on top of the role — exact matches only from the PERMISSIONS catalog; wildcards (* or module.*) are forbidden (Phase D-1 lock)
tips.4: HR fields: employment_type / work_location / payment_type / salary / hire_date — these appear on the payslip
tips.5: You cannot delete the last admin — the system blocks it
tips.6: Deactivating an account (status=inactive) is better than deleting — it preserves history
tips.8: If you're hiring a new employee and need to auto-generate an offer letter PDF, an NDA, and an asset handover form, use the onboarding wizard at /dashboard/hr/onboarding instead of adding a user directly here.
tips.9: Employees created via the onboarding wizard show a "Created via onboarding" marker on their row, linking directly to the onboarding record.

dashboard_roles.goal: Create custom roles with specific permissions for each module in the system. Roles are the auditable way to grant broad permissions (wildcards). Individual per-employee permissions belong in extra_permissions (no wildcards).

tips.0: System roles (is_system=true) cannot be deleted — admin + employee + sales_agent
tips.1: Create a custom role: Arabic + English name + color + icon + a selected list of permissions
tips.2: Permissions are grouped by module in an organized catalog (lib/auth/rbac.ts PERMISSION_CATALOG)
tips.3: Every role automatically inherits BASE_EMPLOYEE (self-service HR — leave/attendance/timesheet/payroll.view) — no need to worry about them
tips.4: The `*` wildcard (super-admin) or `module.*` must live in the role, never in extra_permissions
tips.5: Changing permissions takes effect immediately — every user with the same role gets the change on their next request
tips.6: The ROLE_EXTRAS map in rbac.ts defines the legacy roles; custom DB roles take precedence over it

dashboard_permissions.goal: Control who can access specific files and folders. Assign read, write, delete, and share permissions to each user or team.

tips.0: Assign permissions to a user or team on a specific folder
tips.1: You can set an expiry date for a temporary permission
tips.2: Permissions: read, write, delete, share
tips.3: Delete a permission to remove a user's access to a given folder

dashboard_reports.description: Comprehensive reports on projects, clients, and team
dashboard_reports.goal: The general reports hub: project reports, client reports, storage, revenue, and team performance.

tips.0: Choose the report type from the available cards
tips.1: Every report shows up-to-date data
tips.2: Reports can be exported as files

dashboard_automations.description: Set up workflow automation and notifications
dashboard_automations.goal: Automate repetitive tasks: send automatic notifications, update statuses, and run actions based on specific events.

tips.0: Create a new automation rule with a condition and a trigger
tips.1: Choose the action: notification, status update, or webhook
tips.2: Track the execution log for each rule
tips.3: You can temporarily disable a rule without deleting it

dashboard_knowledge_base.description: Knowledge base for FAQs and documentation
dashboard_knowledge_base.goal: Create knowledge articles for clients and the team. Articles appear in the client portal as a help center.

tips.0: Create categories to organize articles
tips.1: Published articles appear in the client portal
tips.2: Use the editor to format articles easily

dashboard_integrations.goal: Connect the workspace to external services (Slack, Zapier, etc.) via webhooks to exchange data automatically.

tips.0: Create a new webhook with the external service's URL
tips.1: Choose the events you want to send
tips.2: Track the delivery log and the success/failure of each request

dashboard_activity.description: Comprehensive log of all system activities
dashboard_activity.goal: Monitor everything happening in the workspace: from file uploads to project edits and invoice creation.

tips.0: Filter by user or activity type
tips.1: Activities are sorted from newest to oldest
tips.2: Click an activity to see the details

dashboard_login_history.goal: Track who logged in, when, and where, to keep accounts secure.

tips.0: Watch for suspicious login attempts
tips.1: Track each user's last activity
tips.2: Geographic locations appear when available

dashboard_sessions.goal: Browse active sessions and end any suspicious session to protect accounts.

tips.0: Active sessions show device and browser information
tips.1: You can end any user's session
tips.2: The current session is marked and cannot be ended from here

dashboard_admin_error_logs.goal: Browse the errors captured by the observability collector from APIs, crons, webhooks, and client-side error boundaries. Start with the "unresolved" filter to focus on what needs attention. PII is sanitized, with automatic 90-day retention.

tips.0: Triage workflow: start with "unresolved" + "production" + "7 days" → inspect → mark resolved with notes → repeat
tips.1: Click any row to see the full stack trace + request_headers + request_body + user context
tips.2: Severity filter: critical/error/warning/info — start with critical + error
tips.3: PII is sanitized before storage — it appears as [EMAIL] / [PHONE] / [REDACTED]
tips.4: Arabic-Indic numerals (٠-٩) + spaced/parens-formatted phone numbers are sanitized too (Phase D-3 hardening)
tips.5: Retention is 90 days — a daily 03:00 Dubai cron automatically deletes older rows (Phase D-3)
tips.6: The "production" filter hides noise coming from the local development environment
tips.7: Every change (resolve / reopen) is recorded on the row with resolved_by + resolved_at + resolved_notes
tips.8: Two separate permissions: error_logs.view (read) + error_logs.manage (PATCH/edit) — admin only

dashboard_settings.goal: Customize workspace settings: company information + business entities (multi-license) + invoice/quote prefixes + banking details + WhatsApp instances + agent WhatsApp routing + portal + API keys.

tips.0: "General" tab: default company data + multiple business entities (each invoice/quote picks an entity) + prefixes + banking details
tips.1: "API Keys" tab: create keys for external integrations (n8n crons, Telegram bot, Evolution webhook). Each key has a permissions list — use '*' for a super-key or specify cron.<name> for crons
tips.2: "WhatsApp Instances" tab: create + connect (QR code) + disconnect — each instance stores status + last_connected_at
tips.3: "Agent WhatsApp Settings" tab: link each agent to an instance + recipient_phone — used by the follow-up reminders cron (Phase 11 Refinement)
tips.4: "Portal" tab: welcome message + client branding customization
tips.5: "System Settings" tab: quick links to sales, leave, evaluation, leave-types settings, etc.
tips.6: Changes take effect immediately after saving — no restart needed
tips.7: API keys must be stored externally (n8n credential) — there is no way to retrieve them after creation (only a hash is shown)

dashboard_crm.goal: View sales statistics: total leads, new leads, converted leads, pending follow-ups, and pipeline distribution.

tips.0: The top cards show a quick summary of sales status
tips.1: The sales pipeline shows how leads are distributed across stages
tips.2: Recent leads shows the newest additions
tips.3: Use reports to identify your best lead sources

dashboard_crm_pipeline.description: Manage sales leads with Kanban and table views
dashboard_crm_pipeline.goal: The full lead lifecycle in one place: from creation, through qualification across the pipeline stages, to conversion into an actual client. Drag leads between stages or open any lead to see all its details.

tips.0: Lifecycle: create ("+ New Lead" button) → qualify (drag between stages) → close (closed_won requires admin approval) → convert ("Convert to Client" button on the lead page)
tips.1: Use Kanban to drag leads between stages — mobile uses the "Move Stage" button instead of dragging
tips.2: A pending closed_won stage waits for admin approval — it moves to approved (auto-converts) or rejected
tips.3: Admin can filter by priority + source + assigned agent; an agent only sees their own leads (strict scoping)
tips.4: Every lead opens a detail page with 6 tabs: overview, activity, tasks, deals, attachments, notes
tips.5: Click "Link to Existing Client" to link a lead to a client already in the system (used when the client has purchased before)
tips.6: Reassignment (admin only): from the lead page use "Change Owner", or for bulk reassignment click "Select Multiple" in the Pipeline, pick several leads, then "Assign To". The new owner gets a notification and the change is logged in the activity timeline — in both cases

dashboard_crm_leads_archived.goal: Archived leads are hidden from the pipeline and lists but not permanently deleted. From here you can review them and restore any of them. Archiving itself is done from the "Archive" button on the lead detail page.

tips.0: Archiving (admin or anyone with the leads.delete permission): from the lead detail page → the admin actions row → "Archive" button → confirm
tips.1: An archived lead disappears instantly from the pipeline and lists (without permanent deletion) — you can restore it at any time
tips.2: Restore: from this page click the "Restore" button next to the lead → it goes back to the normal pipeline
tips.3: Each row shows the archive date and who archived it; click the name to open the lead's details
tips.4: An agent only sees their own archived leads (strict scoping); admin sees all of them

dashboard_calendar.description: Unified calendar over tasks, follow-ups, and meetings
dashboard_calendar.goal: A unified calendar combining 3 sources in one view: lead tasks (with a due date) + follow-ups + meetings logged as activity. The calendar is read-only — clicking any event opens the source for editing in its natural place.

tips.0: 4 views: month (default on desktop) / week / day / agenda (default on mobile)
tips.1: Click a day number in month view to jump to day view; click the day name in week view for the same
tips.2: The filters (tasks/follow-ups/meetings) hide the types you don't need — the URL remembers your selection
tips.3: The URL remembers the view + date + filters — copy it or use the browser's back button
tips.4: Clicking any event takes you to its source: task → the Tasks tab / follow-up → Overview with highlight / meeting → Activity with highlight
tips.5: An overdue event appears in red; a completed one shows a strikethrough + reduced opacity
tips.6: The calendar always uses Dubai time (+04:00) — no DST
tips.7: The maximum date range is 62 days — the API rejects anything larger (to avoid huge queries)

dashboard_crm_leads_id_tab_tasks.goal: Track the tasks tied to each lead — a follow-up call, preparing a quote, sending an email. Lead tasks are independent from board tasks — each stays in its natural place. They appear in "My Tasks" + the calendar automatically.

tips.0: Quick create: click "+ Add Task" (h-11 touch target) → type the title → pick a due date + priority → "Add"
tips.1: Quick status toggle: click the checkbox — it switches between pending ↔ completed (completed items move to the end automatically)
tips.2: Quick title edit: click the task title → type the edit → Enter to save or Escape to cancel
tips.3: Edit all details: click the ⋮ (3-dot) menu → "Edit Details" → opens a sheet with every field
tips.4: Delete: ⋮ → "Delete" → confirm (admin or the task's creator only — no window.confirm, a proper dialog)
tips.5: Priorities: urgent (red) / high (orange) / medium (amber) / low (gray) — no priority hides the chip
tips.6: Due dates: overdue by N days (red) / today (orange) / tomorrow (amber) / in N days (yellow) / an absolute date (gray)
tips.7: Tasks appear in "My Tasks" with a sticky-note icon + a "Lead" tag to distinguish them from board tasks
tips.8: Tasks appear in the calendar's unified view — clicking a task in the calendar brings you back here

dashboard_crm_leads_id_tab_activity.goal: A complete timeline of everything that happened on the lead: notes, calls, meetings, stage changes, file attachments, and any field update. You can mention colleagues with @username and they'll get a notification + a direct link to the mentioned activity.

tips.0: Add a quick note: type in the composer above the timeline → choose the type (note/call/meeting/email) → save
tips.1: @-mentions: type @ then the employee's name (instant search across admin + sales agents); after posting, they get a notification + a direct link
tips.2: The link in the notification opens here with an automatic scroll + an orange flash highlight on the mentioned activity (for two seconds)
tips.3: Every activity has a distinct icon + color: note (sticky note), call (phone), meeting (calendar clock), email (mail)
tips.4: System activities (stage_change, lead_created, etc.) are written automatically — you can't add them manually
tips.5: Cursor pagination: click "Load More" to see older activities (50 per page)

dashboard_crm_leads_id_tab_files.goal: Attach images + voice notes to any lead. Designed mobile-first (Sayed on iPhone) — direct camera capture, voice recording, or picking from the gallery.

tips.0: Camera 📷: opens the phone's camera directly (capture=environment) — no need to save the photo and re-upload it
tips.1: Gallery 🖼️: pick more than one photo at once (supports HEIC on iOS — converted to JPEG automatically)
tips.2: Voice recording 🎤: click to start → a pulsing indicator appears + an MM:SS counter; click Stop to save or Cancel to discard
tips.3: Hard limits: 5 MB per file (after automatic image resize to 1920×1920) + 10 attachments per lead (shared between images and voice)
tips.4: Images are saved with EXIF stripped (geo-metadata is wiped automatically)
tips.5: Voice recording limit: 5 minutes (auto-stops at 4:30 with a warning) — enough for a quick note
tips.6: Delete: admin or the original uploader only
tips.7: Files open in a side sheet for the full view + preview + a download button

dashboard_sales_chat.goal: Communicate with clients over WhatsApp directly from the system. Messages are stored locally and automatically linked to leads.

tips.0: Select a conversation from the list to view its messages
tips.1: Messages refresh automatically every 5 seconds
tips.2: Incoming messages are automatically linked to a lead by phone number

dashboard_sales_approvals.goal: Review and approve quotes submitted by sales staff before they're sent to clients.

tips.0: Quotes from sales staff need admin approval before sending
tips.1: You can add a comment along with approving or rejecting
tips.2: Once approved, the quote becomes ready to send

dashboard_crm_follow_ups.goal: All scheduled follow-ups with leads in one place — upcoming, overdue, completed. Every follow-up has a due time + an optional WhatsApp reminder that reaches the assigned agent 30 minutes before the appointment.

tips.0: Basic workflow: create (from the lead page) → receive a reminder 30 minutes before → do the follow-up → click "Done"
tips.1: Icons: upcoming (blue clock) / today (orange) / overdue (red) / completed (green checkmark) / cancelled (gray X)
tips.2: The WhatsApp reminder goes to the assigned AGENT (not the client) — it requires Phase 11.5 routing configured in settings
tips.3: Quick create from the central page or from the lead's own page
tips.4: Quick complete: a single click on ✓ in the row; triggers an optimistic update + invalidates the React Query cache
tips.5: Follow-ups appear in the calendar automatically + in the "My Calendar" widget on the dashboard
tips.6: Snooze is still in v1.1 — for now you can only complete or cancel

dashboard_sales_whatsapp_analytics.description: WhatsApp conversation analytics and performance metrics
dashboard_sales_whatsapp_analytics.goal: View customer satisfaction (CSAT) and service level (SLA) statistics for WhatsApp conversations, with date filtering.

tips.0: Use the date filter to compare performance across periods
tips.1: The CSAT rating shows average satisfaction and the rating breakdown per agent
tips.2: SLA shows compliance with first-response time and resolution time

dashboard_sales_settings.goal: Manage pipeline stages + tags + linking WhatsApp numbers to staff + canned responses + SLA policies. Note: this will fold into /dashboard/crm/settings in v1.1.

tips.0: Pipeline stages: custom order + colors + a minimum expected-value threshold (for qualification)
tips.1: closed_won = a special stage — it requires admin approval before auto-converting to a client
tips.2: Lead classification: source (manual / facebook / instagram / whatsapp / referral / etc.) + priority + tags
tips.3: WhatsApp instances: link each employee to a dedicated instance (1-to-1)
tips.4: Agent WhatsApp Settings (Phase 11 Refinement): more flexible — a shared instance can serve multiple agents, each with a different recipient_phone
tips.5: Use the QR code for the initial WhatsApp instance connection — it opens a 60-second waiting window
tips.6: Canned responses: quick message templates for staff in the WhatsApp shared inbox
tips.7: SLA policies: first-response time and resolution time limits for each conversation priority

dashboard_admin_backup_procedure.goal: How to take backups of the Postgres database with GPG encryption + the restore procedure. The procedure is manual and fully documented in docs/MIGRATIONS.md §10.

tips.0: Backup command: pnpm db:backup [<label>] — example: pnpm db:backup pre-019
tips.1: Output lands in backups/<TS>_<label>.sql.gz or .sql.gz.gpg (gitignored)
tips.2: Encryption is opt-in: set BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_PASSPHRASE in .env.local to enable GPG AES256 (Phase D-4)
tips.3: gpg must be installed: brew install gnupg (mac) / apt install gnupg (linux) / Gpg4win (windows)
tips.4: Restoring an encrypted backup: gpg --batch --quiet --decrypt --passphrase-fd 3 --output - "$FILE" 3<<<"$PASS" | gunzip | psql "$URL"
tips.5: Restoring an unencrypted (legacy) backup: gunzip -c "$FILE" | psql "$URL"
tips.6: Safe retention — manually keep important backups (before big migrations) off-machine until offsite storage lands in v1.1

dashboard_admin_security_checklist.goal: A quick security reference: the locked patterns in CLAUDE.md + the practices required of admin. Read this before changing any security-sensitive routes.

tips.0: extra_permissions is exact-match only — wildcards (*, module.*) are forbidden in the UI + API (Phase D-1)
tips.1: Search input in APIs must go through escapePostgrestValue(escapeLike(...)) — any custom regex strip is an injection risk (Phase D-1)
tips.2: Auth endpoints (login + reset + 2FA) are protected by a dual rate limiter: IP + email account lockout (Phase D-2)
tips.3: Every secret comparison must use timingSafeEqual with a length-guard before it (Phase 14.3 + D-4)
tips.4: Backups have an optional passphrase — but with real data, enable it (Phase D-4)
tips.5: Error logs have automatic 90-day retention + 5-layer PII redaction (Phase 14.1 + D-3)
tips.6: The minimum password length is 8 characters, unified across the system (Phase 14.3 #5 lock)
tips.7: Task descriptions are plain text only — no markdown rendering (Phase 14.3 XSS fix)
tips.8: If you see data in error logs that looks sensitive, flag the row "needs PII review" in notes
tips.9: 2FA is currently disabled (deferred to v1.1 — needs encrypt + enforce together)

ui.pageSubtitle: Explanation of all system modules and how to use them — {count} modules
ui.noResults: No results — try a different search term
